title: unable to start machine on M4 based MacOS system
body:
### Issue Description

I try to get podman running on my new M4 based MacOS build agent.

Anythings goes fine until I try to start the machine. podman says that it's  starting "podman-machine-default" but this never returns. I waited up to an hour without any reaction from podman.


```
runner04@mini08 ~ % podman --version
podman version 5.3.1

runner04@mini08 ~ % podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.3.1/libpod/_ping": dial unix /tmp/storage-run-506/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory

runner04@mini08 ~ % podman machine init
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.3 to create VM
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 13b69bec7030 done   |
Copying config 44136fa355 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
13b69bec70305ccd85d47a0bd6d2357381c957cf87dceb862427aace4b964a2b
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-arm64.raw: done
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start

runner04@mini08 ~ % podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
^C%

runner04@mini08 ~ % podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49776: connect: connection refused
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. brew install podman
2. podman machine init
3. podman machine start


### Describe the results you received

No result at all from command `podman machine start`, process hangs forever without reaction until I kill it.

### Describe the results you expected

Best result would podman successfully starts the machine without issues.
At least I would expect that podman returns with a failure.

### podman info output

```yaml
runner04@mini08 ~ % podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49776: connect: connection refused
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

    System Software Overview:

      System Version: macOS 15.1 (24B2083)
      Kernel Version: Darwin 24.1.0
      Boot Mode: Normal
      Computer Name: mini08
      User Name: runner04 (runner04)
      Secure Virtual Memory: Enabled
      System Integrity Protection: Enabled
      Time since boot: 18 Stunden und 38 Minuten

    Hardware Overview:

      Model Name: Mac mini
      Model Identifier: Mac16,11
      Chip: Apple M4 Pro
      Total Number of Cores: 12 (8 performance and 4 efficiency)
      Memory: 48 GB
      System Firmware Version: 11881.41.5
      OS Loader Version: 11881.41.5


### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

Hi @bschramke! Installing podman via `brew` is not a recommended method. 

Can you try installing from source, pkginstaller, or using one of our binaries in the Releases page and seeing if this is still an issue? You can find the documentation for that here: https://podman.io/docs/installation

Hi @jakecorrenti 

tried with podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg and podman-installer-macos-universal.pkg.. both fail to install

couple of questions:

1. Did you fully uninstall podman from brew before installing with the installer?
2. If so, could you provide the same transcript for `podman machine start` with `podman --log-level debug machine start` and perhaps we can see whats going on.

> 1. Did you fully uninstall podman from brew before installing with the installer?

&& rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers 
&& rm -rf ~/.config/containers
